_title: NFPA 70 Articles 700-705 — Emergency Systems, Legally Required Standby, Optional Standby, Interconnected Sources
_note: Bundle of four closely-related articles. Each addresses a different service-continuity tier — Art 700 (life safety, ≤10 s restoration), Art 701 (legally required, ≤60 s), Art 702 (optional, owner-elected), Art 705 (interconnection of multiple sources).

articles.article_title: Emergency Systems / Legally Required Standby / Optional Standby / Interconnected Power Production Sources
articles.scope: Article 700: emergency systems essential for safety to human life (egress lighting, fire alarm, refrigeration of critical med, etc.) — restoration ≤10 s. Article 701: legally required standby (HVAC for life safety, sewage, communications, industrial process) — restoration ≤60 s. Article 702: optional standby (owner-elected — comfort, computer protection). Article 705: interconnection of multiple sources (PV, wind, generator, utility).

key_sections.title: Capacity
key_sections.summary: Sized to carry all emergency loads simultaneously

key_sections.title: Wiring, Emergency System
key_sections.summary: Independent wiring from emergency source to emergency loads; SEPARATE from normal-source wiring

key_sections.title: General Requirements (Sources of Power)
key_sections.summary: Emergency power source options: storage battery, generator set, uninterruptible power supply (UPS), separate utility service, fuel cell

key_sections.title: Coordination — Emergency
key_sections.summary: Emergency system OCPDs shall be selectively coordinated with all supply-side OCPDs (overcurrent that would otherwise open emergency circuits)

key_sections.title: Capacity — Legally Required
key_sections.summary: Capacity for all legally required standby loads simultaneously

key_sections.title: Wiring, Legally Required Standby
key_sections.summary: Per Art 215 / 220 — no special separation requirement (unlike Art 700)

key_sections.title: Selective Coordination — Legally Required Standby
key_sections.summary: Same selectivity rule as 700.32 applied to legally required standby

key_sections.title: Capacity and Rating — Optional Standby
key_sections.summary: Sized per the loads it serves — no NEC-mandated capacity minimum

key_sections.title: Transfer Equipment
key_sections.summary: Transfer equipment listed for the intended purpose (UL 1008 for ATS / manual transfer switches)

key_sections.title: Equipment Disconnect
key_sections.summary: Disconnecting means for each power source; lockable, identified

key_sections.title: Load-Side Source Connections
key_sections.summary: PV/wind/generator interconnection at load side of service disconnect — busbar/feeder ampacity rules (120% rule, sum rule). Most common interconnection method for residential PV

Emergency_vs_Standby_Comparison.title: Article 700 / 701 / 702 — Required Restoration Times and Scope

rows.name: Emergency Systems
rows.scope: Life safety: egress lighting, fire alarm, fire-pump backup, healthcare critical branch, mass transit

rows.name: Legally Required Standby
rows.scope: Communications, industrial process, sewage, HVAC for life safety

rows.name: Optional Standby
rows.scope: Owner-elected: comfort, computer protection, retail HVAC

Load_Side_Interconnection_Rules_705.12.title: PV/Wind Interconnection at Load Side — Busbar Ampacity Rules

rows.description: Inverter output OCPD + main breaker rating ≤ 100% of busbar ampacity
If sum > 100%: inverter output OCPD ≤ (busbar ampacity × 120% − main breaker rating). I.e., main breaker downgrade or busbar upgrade needed
For PV inverter breaker on opposite end from main breaker, allow up to 120% rule without main breaker downgrade
Sum of all overcurrent devices supplying busbar ≤ 100% of busbar ampacity (the conservative rule)

common_errors: Mixing Art 700 emergency circuits with normal-source circuits in shared wiring — Art 700.10 requires independent wiring
Failing selective coordination requirement (700.32, 701.32) — common on retrofit of small commercial buildings adding fire pump
Misapplying 705.12 — assuming the 120% busbar rule applies when actually the sum rule applies (busbar is the limiting factor)
Treating Art 702 as having no NEC requirements — capacity sizing per 702.4 and transfer equipment listing per 702.5 still apply
related_iec_60364: IEC 60364-8-2 (Prosumer's electrical installations) — for interconnection rules
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Articles 700/701/702 distinguish emergency vs legally-required-standby vs optional-standby with explicit restoration times (10 s / 60 s / not specified); IEC 60364-5-56 has 'safety services' (corresponds roughly to NEC 700) and 'standby services' (corresponds to NEC 701/702 combined) with different restoration classifications (Class 0 = no break, 0.15 s, 0.5 s, 15 s, > 15 s). NEC 705.12 interconnection rules (sum / 120% / center-fed) are very prescriptive; IEC 60364-8-2 has equivalent requirements but less geometrically specific. Selective coordination requirement (700.32 / 701.32) is more explicit in NEC; IEC handles via IEC 60364-5-53 device coordination tables.
articles.usage_notes: Always identify which article applies (700 vs 701 vs 702) based on what the loads ARE — emergency systems are by code authority (life safety), legally required by other AHJ/code mandate, optional by owner. Document selective coordination on critical fire-pump and life-safety circuits — frequent inspection failure.
